fields.title: Research on skeletal system applying morphometrics and artificial intelligence approaches
fields.keywords: bones, machine learning, 3D imaging, geometric morphometrics
fields.description:
The main objective of the project is to seek new knowledge about sex- and age-specific morphometric characteristics and manifestations of asymmetry in the skeletal system of the contemporary Bulgarian population. This is carried out using 3D imaging through non-invasive virtual anatomical dissections, image processing, segmentation of human body structures, and the application of classical statistical methods, including geometric morphometrics, as well as artificial intelligence algorithms for data analysis and the development of mathematical models for intelligent problem-solving in various classes of biomedical tasks.
The multidisciplinary approaches for data collection and analysis contribute to a deeper understanding of the relationships between shape, size, and the manifestation of asymmetry in selected skeletal structures, as well as their association with biological characteristics such as sex and age in the contemporary Bulgarian population.

fields.title: Investigation of human anatomic variations on three-dimensional models using innovative and interdisciplinary approaches and organization of a Virtual Anatomy Collection
fields.description: The project focuses on advancing the study of anatomical variations of the human skull through innovative and interdisciplinary approaches grounded in three-dimensional modeling. Its main aims are as follows: 1) To investigate anatomical variations of the skull using 3D volume and polygonal models generated through different digitization technologies; 2) To perform comparative qualitative and quantitative analyses by applying statistical methods of geometric morphometrics; 3) To develop an ontology of skull anatomical variations that defines taxonomies, hierarchies, and relationships among variations; 4) To create a Virtual Anatomy Collection based on Linked Open Data that serves as a structured, interoperable database; 5) To enable the application of data mining methods for identifying patterns and regularities in the etiology and localization of anatomical variations.

fields.title: Virtual anthropology – an innovative and interdisciplinary approach for investigations in the field of biomedicine
fields.description: The project includes the following tasks: 1) Validation of a method for sex determination in Bulgarians based on metric characteristics of the skull by the development of discriminant functions; 2) Development of a new method for estimation of the physiological age based on a quantitative assessment of the degree of suture obliteration; 3) Creation of database and development of sex and age specific standards of the soft tissue thicknesses for the Bulgarian population and adaptation of a method for virtual reconstruction of the soft tissues on the skull; 4) Development of advanced methods for image processing of medical CT data. The project combines interdisciplinary approaches, applied for the first time in Bulgaria.
